summary: Replace hanging twining-mcp --version with npm list + 3s timeout in captureEnvironment()
context: The benchmark harness CLI was hanging indefinitely on startup. Traced to captureEnvironment() calling execSync('twining-mcp --version') — the twining-mcp binary doesn't support --version, it starts an MCP server + dashboard and never exits, blocking the entire process.
rationale: Replaced with npm list twining-mcp (which returns package info without starting the server) and added a 3-second timeout as a safety net. The version string is only used for metadata/reproducibility, not runtime behavior.

alternatives.option: Add timeout to the original execSync call
alternatives.reason_rejected: twining-mcp --version fundamentally starts a server — even with timeout it would leave orphan processes
Version info in run metadata aids reproducibility, worth keeping if we can get it safely
